agfFormatVersion.description: AGF project format version. Bumped on breaking schema changes; absence is treated as version 1 with a deprecation warning.

skyGradient.description: S52 POLISH-shadows-bench-sky: vertical sky gradient. When set, the adapter builds a 4×256 CanvasTexture (top→bottom interpolated) and assigns it as `scene.background`, overriding the solid `background` colour. Cheap visual upgrade — the IBL environment (`scene.environment`) still drives PBR ambient; the gradient is purely the skybox the camera sees behind everything.

post.description: M21-post-pipeline: ordered post-processing chain. Each entry is a single pass. The adapter always appends an OutputPass after the configured chain so tone-mapping + sRGB conversion fire at the end.

oneOf.description: S57 POST-ssao: screen-space ambient occlusion via `three/addons/postprocessing/SSAOPass`. Adds a deferred-style AO pre-pass; cost roughly 5–10 % of base render at default settings.
S57 POST-color-lut: final-pass colour grading via a 3D `.cube` LUT loaded by `three/addons/postprocessing/LUTPass`. The lookup runs in linear space before tone-mapping.

file.description: Project-relative path to a `.cube` LUT file under `assetRoot`.

color.description: M21-color: output color pipeline. WebGLRenderer's outputColorSpace stays at the engine default (sRGB); `toneMapping` controls how HDR colour ranges fold back into LDR. Default `none` (legacy linear clamp) — opt in to `aces-filmic` / `agx` for PBR-correct highlight roll-off.

exposure.description: Multiplier on the scene luminance before tone mapping. >1 brightens, <1 darkens.

transmissionResolutionScale.description: Multiplier on the resolution of WebGLRenderer's transmission pre-pass. Three.js renders the whole opaque scene into a private render target every frame so `MeshPhysicalMaterial.transmission` can sample refraction. Lower values (e.g. 0.5) halve that pre-pass cost — usually invisible because the result is mip-blurred by the material's roughness anyway.

shadows.description: Optional shadow-pipeline config. CSM (Cascade Shadow Maps) replaces the per-light directional shadow with N depth cascades sized to the active camera frustum — required for large outdoor scenes where one shadow map cannot cover the visible range without acne.

autoUpdate.description: M21-shadow-static: when false, `renderer.shadowMap.autoUpdate` is disabled and the map only re-renders on `__agf.renderer.invalidateShadowMap()`. Use for scenes whose shadow casters never move (large outdoor maps, RTS games) — saves a per-frame shadow pass per cascade.

algorithm.description: M21-shadow-algorithm: shadow-map filtering. `pcf` (default) — 4-tap percentage-closer filter, sharp + universally supported. `vsm` — variance shadow maps, smoother penumbras but light-leaks on concave geometry. `pcss` — percentage-closer soft shadows via shader-chunk substitution; smoothest penumbras + distance-aware blur. PCSS substitution is process-wide and one-way (toggling back needs a reload).

csm.description: M21-shadow-csm: cascade shadow maps via three/addons/csm/CSM.js. When enabled, every material the adapter manages has `csm.setupMaterial(material)` called on it; existing directional Light entities should set castShadow: false (CSM brings its own directional lights). AGF_CSM_DIRECTIONAL_CONFLICT diagnostic fires when the scene still has a shadow-casting directional light.

shadowNormalBias.description: Normal-space bias applied to each cascade's DirectionalLightShadow. Counters peter-pan (visible gap between an object and its shadow at the contact point) without growing acne the way a more-negative shadowBias would.

idleMode.description: S54 RUNTIME-idle-rendering. `always` (default): the runtime calls `renderer.render()` every animation frame. `on-demand`: the runtime skips the render call when the ECS mutation counter is unchanged from the previous frame (no entity add/remove, no component write). Useful for inspector / menu / wallpaper scenes where the camera is parked and nothing animates. First frame after boot, every window resize, and every frame any system writes ECS state always render.

criticalAssets.description: S54 RUNTIME-progressive-loading: asset refs (`runtime/materials/hero.material.json`, `runtime/models/hero.glb`, …) that must finish loading before `window.__agf.rendererReady` resolves. Every other asset stays on the existing placeholder-then-swap path. Tests / dev-bridge / Playwright `await` `rendererReady` instead of guessing how many frames the first draw takes.

auto.description: S53: default flipped to true — BatchingSystem treats every entity with a built-in primitive mesh (box / sphere / plane) and no LOD / scene-extension overrides as Batchable without needing an explicit `Batchable` component. Set to false to keep the legacy single-Mesh path. Per-entity opt-out (independent of the global flag): `Batchable: { enabled: false }`.

path.description: S51/S53 project-wide bucket path default. `instanced` (default) — one InstancedMesh per (mesh + material + shadow + group) with whole-bucket frustum culling. `batched` — one BatchedMesh per (material + shadow + group); each geometry registers once and three.js does per-instance frustum culling on the CPU with an O(N) loop. `batched-bvh` (S53) — same as `batched` but builds a BVH over the per-instance bounding boxes; frustum culling becomes a tree walk instead of the linear loop, expected to flip the InstancedMesh vs BatchedMesh crossover toward smaller scenes. Override per-entity via `Batchable: { path: "batched-bvh" }`.

physics.description: Optional Rapier3D physics config (M24). When `enabled: true`, the runtime lazy-imports the Rapier WASM adapter and registers PhysicsSyncSystem in the fixed-update loop. Projects that omit this block pay zero bundle cost.

enabled.description: Master toggle. False (or absent) → no Rapier import, no PhysicsSyncSystem.

fixedDt.description: Physics step in seconds. Defaults to 1/60 — matches the engine's fixed-update tick. Override to slow-mo (0.0083 = 120 Hz) or potato mode (0.033 = 30 Hz).

persistence.description: Optional persistence allowlist. When present, runtime.save() persists only the component values listed here. Absent → save/load throw at runtime.
